chunks.text: The Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering (MSAJCE), established on 5th July 2001 under the aegis of Mohamed Sathak Trust, is approved by AICTE New Delhi, affiliated to Anna University Chennai and Government of Tamil Nadu. MSAJCE strives to continuously upgrade its facilities to provide quality technical education to meet the industrial and societal needs, by providing skill-based training with state of the art facilities. The college campus, sprawling over 70 acres of greenery, is located inside the SIPCOT IT Park, Siruseri, and is surrounded by multinational IT companies such as TCS, CTS, Intellect, Aspire, Steria, Polaris, FSS, etc. MSAJCE has good infrastructure facilities such as spacious classrooms, drawing halls, laboratories, seminar halls, computer facilities with internet and wifi connectivity, library, workshops, auditorium and playground. The college is well connected by Road, Rail and Air from various parts of the country. A team of well qualified and experienced faculty supports the teaching and learning process. The college provides equal importance for academic, co-curricular and extracurricular activities. MSAJCE provides all kinds of training for the professional and overall transformation of the students. Mohamed Sathak Trust, a charitable and educational organization, was established on 26 October 1973 at Kilakarai, by the Philanthropic Mohamed Sathak Family of Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram District, Tamil Nadu, with the sole purpose of helping deprived people from rural and financially challenged backgrounds to obtain quality education. The first self-financing engineering college in the country was established by the Trust in 1984 at Kilakarai. From that day onwards, the Trust has been contributing to provide quality education with academic excellence to meet the requirements of industries at national and international levels. The Trust has an impeccable track record of running quality educational institutions from higher secondary level to undergraduate and postgraduate levels in various disciplines such as Engineering Technology, Arts and Science, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Nursing and Medical Sciences. It has 18 educational institutions at present in Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram and Chennai. The Trust's guiding philosophy: "God will not change the condition of a people until they try to change themselves." The Trust focuses on doing right things without any bias and with a charitable mind to build institutions of academic excellence and help in building the nation. chunks.page_title: 1. Department Overview — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)
chunks.section: 1. Department Overview — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)
chunks.text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the process of creating machines to think like humans. AI is a term used to describe the simulation of human intelligence processes in machines. It typically involves sophisticated algorithms that enable computers to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition and decision making. Data Science is a field of knowledge which applies advanced statistical and mathematical techniques to large datasets in order to extract knowledge from them. Data can be structured like a spreadsheet or unstructured like text or video data. Data science is the application of techniques from statistics, mathematics, computer programming and other fields to solve real-world problems using information technology. Due to the exploration of the Internet, AI and Data Science deliver data-driven solutions using computational principles, methods, and systems for extracting information from data and modern computational systems that reveal capabilities of insight, way of thinking, learning, and action that are typical of human intelligence. B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) has emerged as one of the most high-growth, dynamic, and lucrative careers in technology since 2015. This course aims at providing core technologies such as artificial intelligence, data mining, and data modeling, and also gives intensive inputs in areas of machine learning and big data analytics. Students gain cross-disciplinary skills across fields such as statistics, computer science, machine learning, and logic. Career opportunities exist in healthcare, business, e-commerce, social networking companies, climatology, biotechnology, genetics, and other important areas. The major focus of this programme is to equip students with statistical, mathematical reasoning, machine learning, knowledge discovery, and visualization skills. The programme also deals with core principles and ethical issues in Data Science and AI, and motivates students to explore cutting-edge applications related to Big Data, Neural Networks, and Deep Learning. Applications like Alexa/Siri, Erica/Sophia, Tesla's driverless car, Chat-bot, etc. are becoming common. AI-powered Robots can take over human life-endangering jobs like firefighting, deep-sea oil drilling, and mining works with high mortality rates. Career opportunities for AIDS graduates include roles such as Software Engineer, Software Tester, Application Developer, Information and Multimedia Designer, Medical Information Scientist, Technology Developer, IT Specialist, Technical Consultant, Quality Analyst, Information Network Manager, and Data Quality Analyst across Manufacturing, Software, Research, Education, Defense, Banking & Finance, e-Commerce, Transport and Health Science.

The B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) program is a 4-year regular program or a 3-year lateral entry program. The program consists of 8 semesters for regular entry and 6 semesters for lateral entry. The total intake is 30 seats, with 15 government seats and 15 management seats. Eligibility requires a 10+2 system of education with a pass in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. The curriculum and syllabus follow the 2021 Regulation. The scope for higher studies includes M.E., M.Tech., and M.B.A. The vision is to be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Artificial Intelligence and Data Science users through sustainable practices. The mission includes imparting core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science through innovative teaching and learning methodology. It also includes inculcating critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning, and creativity needed for industry and society, and cultivating students with all-round competencies for career, higher education, and self-employability. The program educational objectives include preparing graduates for analysing, designing, developing, and testing software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements, and they will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms. The program specific outcomes include developing creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies / tools for real-time applications of Industry and applying the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications. The programme outcomes include applying the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. They also include identifying, formulating, reviewing research literature, and analyzing complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles. Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health, safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of information to provide valid conclusions. Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities. Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues and consequent responsibilities relevant to professional engineering practice. Understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings. Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large. Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work, and recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

chunks.section: 2. Vision, Mission, PEOs, PSO & Programme Outcomes (POs)
chunks.text: The Management Quota is 30. The Eligibility is 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. The Curriculum is 2021 Regulation — https://www.msjace-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf. Higher Studies include M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A. The following data is from the Vision, Mission, PEOs, PSO & Programme Outcomes table: To be a Centre of Excellence in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning to create globally competent professionals is the vision. To impart quality education in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning through innovative teaching-learning process is the mission. To provide students with a strong foundation in the core principles and practices of AI and ML, enabling them to solve complex engineering problems is the first PEO. To prepare students to be successful professionals in the field of AI and ML, equipped with the skills and knowledge to adapt to rapidly changing technologies is the second PEO. To encourage students to pursue lifelong learning and research, contributing to the advancement of AI and ML is the third PEO. Graduates will be able to apply their knowledge of AI and ML to develop innovative solutions for real-world problems is the first PSO. Graduates will be able to work effectively in teams and communicate their ideas clearly and concisely is the second PSO. Engineering knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems is the first PO. Problem analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences is the second PO. Design/development of solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations is the third PO. Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions is the fourth PO. Modern tool usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations is the fifth PO. The engineer and society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice is the sixth PO. Environment and sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development is the seventh PO. Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice is the eighth PO. Individual and team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings is the ninth PO. Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions is the tenth PO. Project management and finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments is the eleventh PO. Life-long learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change is the twelfth PO.
